COURTESY COPY OF ALL BRIEFS FILED FOR THE HEARING SHOULD BE DELIVERED TO DEPARTMENT 302 BY MAY 6, 2013, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL REQUESTED CLARIFICATION ON THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFF COLONIAL MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE UNCLEAN HANDS AND ESTOPPEL DEFENSES. DEFENDANT SHOULD PLEAD ULTIMATE FACTS TO SUPPORT THOSE DEFENSES AND CLARIFY THE BASIS FOR THE DEFENSES. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL DEFENSE. SEE RODGERS V. SARGENT CONTROLS & AEROSPACE, 136 CAL. APP. 4TH 82, 88-89 (2006) ("[C]OLLATERAL ESTOPPEL AS A DEFENSE NEED NOT BE ALLEGED IN AN ANSWER OR OTHER RESPONSIVE PLEADING"). IN ANY EVENT, THE FIRST AMENDED ANSWER REFERS TO A PRIOR FEDERAL ANTITRUST ACTION (E.G., 5, 20, 24), AND THOSE FACTS ARE SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THE DEFENSE. EMAILED ORDER WAS SIGNED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar May-10-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lawrence Silver, Esq. of Silver & Field, Tel. 310.477.7640, for plaintiff and moving party via Court Call;
Craig Rutenberg, Esq. and Doreen Shenfeld, Esq. of Manatt Phelps & Phillips, Tel. 310.312.4000, for defendant Dignity Health via Court Call;
Kathy K. Lee, Esq. of Musick Peeler, Tel. 213.629.7623, for defendant Golden Empire Managed Care via Court Call.

RULING: Defendant's counsel requested clarification on the tentative ruling. The matter was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: PLAINTIFF COLONIAL MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'s DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the unclean hands and estoppel defenses. Defendant should plead ultimate facts to support those defenses and clarify the basis for the defenses. The demurrer is overruled as to the collateral estoppel defense. See Rodgers v. Sargent Controls & Aerospace, 136 Cal. App. 4th 82, 88-89 (2006) ("[c]ollateral estoppel as a defense need not be alleged in an answer or other responsive pleading"). In any event, the First Amended Answer refers to a prior federal antitrust action (e.g., 5, 20, 24), and those facts are sufficient to support the defense. Emailed order was signed by the Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
